document_metadata.source_filename: Handout - Boat Exercise - Marina Ltd Seller v2.docx

segments.source_text: The Shore Family are re-visiting Marina Ltd to negotiate the purchase of a new canal boat, which meets the needs of their growing family. On the earlier visit to the marina (a couple of weeks ago) Marina Ltd offered the Shore Family a tentative package deal which included an offer of approximately £42,250 for the family’s current boat, the Halifax 36ft, (a 7year old canal boat, 25,000 miles on the clock, 1 previous owner) in part exchange against a new boat, (Harmony 38ft) which was priced at £72k. Marina Ltd said that the offer was subject to the Shore Family taking out a credit agreement through their agent for the balance of the purchase price.
You did not discuss the final purchase price of the new boat in detail as this was dependent on the type of model chosen, fixtures and fittings and more research was needed by both parties.
This is the last day of your sales quarter. If the team sell one more boat today, you reach your quota and are eligible for a bonus of £250 each.

The selling price of the brand new, Harmony 38ft, must be in excess of the amount your company paid for the boat, which was £64,500. The level of boat discussed was around £72k, but if you can persuade them to go for top of the range model/fixtures/fittings you could be looking at a sale of £78k.

You think their current boat is in average to good condition due to its mileage and wear/tear. Auto-boat magazine give a range of £40 – 48K for the Shore Family current boat, so you think your part exchange figure was generous. If though you can persuade them to take a better model Harmony 38ft then you may look to increase that part exchange figure. You aren’t sure what budget they have available, as they seemed reluctant to disclose this on their last visit, but you think a top of the range model might be within their reach.

You are keen to take the Shore Family current boat as you have another dealer you can sell it onto straight away for £43k. If though you held onto it would probably sell for around £48k on the open market, but with current economic conditions this may be a gamble.

For every credit agreement your team pass onto your credit agent, you receive a commission payment which is 1 % of the about financed, which is shared amongst the sale team. This is a very attractive incentive to your team, who are always looking for extra income. The interest rate offered on a part exchange balance is 8.5%, which is competitive with the rates being offered by banks (around 6%), plus you can arrange for all the paperwork to be completed that day with a provisional acceptance.
This is the last likely prospect of the day and your team want that bonus
Boat Exercise - Marina, V2, June 2018 © CIPS 2018, Page 1 of 2
